figures.caption: Figure 1: Performance comparison on the VFHQ-Test [44] dataset. Metrics marked with ∗ indicate lower is better. DVFace achieves the best results on all metrics.

Table 1: Quantitative comparison of video face restoration models on synthetic datasets (VFHQ-Test and HDTF). The best and second-best results are colored with red and blue. ↑ indicates higher is better, and ↓ indicates lower is better.
Figure 4: Qualitative results on challenging cases of the synthetic datasets. Please zoom in for better visibility.

Figure 2: Visual compressions on ynthetic and real-world datasets (Zoom in for details). For the real-world dataset, the reference image (HQ) is unavailable. DVFace restores faithful videos with sharp details.

Table 2: Quantitative comparison of video face restoration models on real-world datasets (RFV-LQ and Voxceleb2). The best and second-best results are colored with red and blue. ↑ indicates higher is better, and ↓ indicates lower is better.
Figure 5: Qualitative results on challenging real-world dataset. Please zoom in for better visibility.

Figure 3: Overview of DVFace. (a) Overall Framework: DVFace restores low-quality face videos with one-step diffusion and facial priors. (b) Spatio-Temporal Dual-Codebook (STDC): spatial and temporal codebooks extract complementary facial priors. (c) Asymmetric Spatio-Temporal Fusion (ASTF): injecting temporal priors globally and spatial priors locally.
